page: Amazon Web Services m8id.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with a dedicated allocation and a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU scores all ranking in the top 10%. The server's DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth. It also excels in specialized tasks, demonstrating top-tier performance in Geekbench software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. The hardware features Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which drive top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models. Single-core performance, however, sits in the average tier.

Given its bare-metal architecture, massive memory bandwidth, and 11.4 TB of local NVMe storage, this instance offers high resource density for demanding enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput Redis deployments, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing. The lack of a dedicated GPU is offset by strong CPU-based LLM inference capabilities, making it a viable option for certain AI workloads that leverage Intel AMX instructions without requiring GPU hardware.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 192 vCPUs, 96 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. It features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. The processor includes Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, enabling top-tier prompt processing for large language models. While single-core performance is average, its massive multi-threaded capacity and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for enterprise databases, Redis deployments, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. This bare-metal configuration delivers exceptional resource density for data-intensive enterprise workloads.

768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
